description:
Sprint 6 Task 18.3 — archetype-to-critic-preference table. When critic-craft and critic-aesthetic disagree on an overlapping concern (rare; their dimensions are orthogonal by design) the merge step in hooks/scripts/lib/critic-merge.mjs consults this table to pick a winner. 'craft' means the conservative, legibility-first critic wins; 'aesthetic' means the taste judge wins.

Order of resolution:
  1. Exact archetype match  → its preferred_critic applies.
  2. Family-level match (archetypes[].matches_any) → that family's preference.
  3. default preferred_critic applies.

The table is opinionated but not load-bearing — conflicts are <1 per 10-prompt suite in practice. Treat it as a tiebreaker, not a directive.

default.reason: When in doubt, legibility and accessibility win over taste. The aesthetic critic can escalate via severity=blocker on a distinctiveness finding if the design is actively generic.

archetypes.name: dashboard
archetypes.reason: Density + affordance clarity trump novelty. Broken typography on a dashboard is worse than boring typography.

archetypes.name: documentation
archetypes.reason: Readers come for information, not aesthetic thrills. Hierarchy + typography + contrast are load-bearing.

archetypes.name: settings-forms
archetypes.reason: Task-completion flows must not sacrifice clarity for flair. Focus states, affordances, and layout win.

archetypes.name: editorial
archetypes.reason: The design IS the product. Generic editorial loses to distinctive editorial every time.

archetypes.name: marketing
archetypes.reason: First-impression media — distinctiveness is a conversion-level concern, not cosmetic.

archetypes.name: portfolio
archetypes.reason: Evidence of taste is the product. Craft issues can be blocker-escalated but aesthetic owns the floor.

archetypes.name: experimental
archetypes.reason: Deliberate taste choices violate conservative craft norms by design — unless contrast drops below WCAG AA (APCA Lc < 45 body text), in which case the escalation below triggers.

escalation_overrides.condition: When critic-craft's contrast OR accessibility score is below 4 AND severity is blocker, craft wins regardless of archetype. Illegibility is not a taste choice.

When critic-aesthetic flags missing prefers-reduced-motion with severity=blocker, aesthetic wins even on craft-preferred archetypes. Motion accessibility is an aesthetic-owned dimension AND a WCAG concern.
